layers.name: Service Operations Gateway
layers.value: Standard service entry for access, operations, billing, and regulation.

layers.name: AI Service Orchestration
layers.value: Scenario solution packages that business can consume directly.

layers.name: Atomic Capability Engine
layers.value: Componentized capabilities accelerate new scenario delivery.

layers.name: Model Runtime & Scheduling
layers.value: Stable latency, throughput, cost control, and availability for model calls.

layers.name: Knowledge Ops
layers.value: Trusted, traceable, and continuously improving knowledge assets.

layers.name: Model Hub
layers.value: Stable, replaceable, and measurable model capability pool.

layers.name: Platform Foundation
layers.value: Secure, scalable, observable, and auditable foundation.

features.name: Project Registry
features.description: Register seven-layer projects with tech stack, owners, and entrypoints.

features.name: Task Planning
features.description: Epic/Story/Task planning with dependencies and milestones.

features.name: Incremental Progress
features.description: Progress tracking driven by Git activity and milestone status.

features.name: Build/Test Orchestration
features.description: Unified build and test runners for Java and Python services.

features.name: Boot Sequence
features.description: Single command to bring up individual layers or the whole stack.

features.name: Audit & Approvals
features.description: Record all control actions and require approvals for risky operations.

features.name: Observability Hooks
features.description: Standard hooks for logs, metrics, traces, and quality gates.

features.name: Architecture & Docs
features.description: Generate and maintain ARCHITECTURE.md and docs index.

features.name: Time Window Metric Aggregation
features.description: L1 gateway should support querying persisted metrics with time-window aggregation so operations can inspect recent traffic quality without parsing raw logs.
acceptance_criteria: Gateway exposes a metrics query interface that supports at least one explicit time window parameter.
Returned data includes aggregated totals, authorized or rejected counts, and latency summary for the requested window.
Overview documentation explains how the time window query is used by operations staff.

features.name: Audit And Metrics Retention Policy
features.description: L1 gateway should archive audit and metric logs with a unified directory and rotation policy so long-running operations stay traceable without unbounded local growth.
acceptance_criteria: Audit and metrics files are written under one documented operations data directory.
Rotation or retention rules are implemented and documented for both audit and metrics logs.
Operational recovery steps describe where current and archived records are stored.

features.name: Operational Health Score
features.description: L1 gateway overview should include a health score derived from upstream status, rejection patterns, and recent failures so operators can assess service condition at a glance.
acceptance_criteria: Overview endpoint returns an explicit health score field with a documented scoring rule.
Scoring rule considers upstream health and recent gateway errors rather than static configuration only.
Quality rubric defines what score ranges require operator follow-up.

features.name: Operations Console
features.description: L1 should provide a web operations console that exposes global overview, governance, audit, quality, and layer-level operations for L2-L7.
acceptance_criteria: Console exposes at least Dashboard and Operations pages.
Dashboard shows seven-layer health, traffic, and alert summary.
Operations page exposes routing, auth, quota, and audit views.

features.name: Debug Studio
features.description: L1 should provide a debugging workspace for request construction, contract mapping inspection, trace lookup, and replay across L2-L7.
acceptance_criteria: Debug Studio can send requests to qa, compliance, and pricing through L1.
Debug Studio displays original request, transformed contract, and downstream response.
Debug Studio supports trace lookup or replay by request identifier.
